chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now like we have arithmetic and relational operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we have one more branch of operators which are called assignment operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in assignment operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the simplest operator is equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whenever in program we have written a equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so it means we are storing the value of b in a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: or we are assigning it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in variables I can write age is equal to 30

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it means we are assigning 30 to our variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: that is why it is called an assignment variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is the simplest assignment variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: apart from this there are many other assignment variables

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like for example we also have plus equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now what is the meaning of plus equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like we have written a statement

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a equals 5 and then we have written a equals a plus 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so what will it mean?

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it means we are storing a equals 5 in a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so earlier 5 was stored in a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but now we have stored 6 in a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we have stored plus 1 in a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so whenever we write a statement like this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: when we want to add or minus something in the same variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and store it in the same variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so there is a short form way to write this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: there is a short form way to write this particular statement

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a plus equal to 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so both of these statements are equivalent

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: they both have the same meaning

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so whenever I want to write a equals a plus 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can write it as a plus equal to 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like for example we have a variable a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now we want to write a equals a plus 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and then we will print the value of a

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here the value of a is 10

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and here the value of a will be 15

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we know that the answer is going to be 15

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now instead of writing this statement we can write

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a plus equal to 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and the answer is going to be same which is equal to 15

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like we have plus equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we have minus equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: minus equal to means a is equal to a minus 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the answer of this will be 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can also tally this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we will get 5 answer

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like we write minus equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can also multiply it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this statement basically means

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a equals to a multiplied by 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the answer will be 50k equal

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we can also divide it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so a equals to a divided by 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the answer will be 2k equal 2.0

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so these are the same values

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we have modulo and power operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so all of these are called assignment operators

text: now like we have arithmetic and relational operators similarly we have one more branch of operators which are called assignment operators in assignment operators the simplest operator is equal to whenever in program we have written a equal to b so it means we are storing the value of b in a or we are assigning it so in variables I can write age is equal to 30 it means we are assigning 30 to our variable that is why it is called an assignment variable so this is the simplest assignment variable apart from this there are many other assignment variables like for example we also have plus equal to now what is the meaning of plus equal to like we have written a statement a equals 5 and then we have written a equals a plus 1 so what will it mean? it means we are storing a equals 5 in a so earlier 5 was stored in a but now we have stored 6 in a so we have stored plus 1 in a so whenever we write a statement like this when we want to add or minus something in the same variable and store it in the same variable so there is a short form way to write this there is a short form way to write this particular statement a plus equal to 1 so both of these statements are equivalent they both have the same meaning so whenever I want to write a equals a plus 1 we can write it as a plus equal to 1 like for example we have a variable a now we want to write a equals a plus 5 and then we will print the value of a so here the value of a is 10 and here the value of a will be 15 so we know that the answer is going to be 15 now instead of writing this statement we can write a plus equal to 5 and the answer is going to be same which is equal to 15 like we have plus equal to similarly we have minus equal to minus equal to means a is equal to a minus 5 so the answer of this will be 5 we can also tally this so we will get 5 answer like we write minus equal to we can also multiply it so this statement basically means a equals to a multiplied by 5 so the answer will be 50k equal similarly we can also divide it so a equals to a divided by 5 so the answer will be 2k equal 2.0 so these are the same values similarly we have modulo and power operator so all of these are called assignment operators
